title: Preparing French Creek data for stream metabolism modeling
text: Stream metabolism models estimate gross primary production ecosystem respiration continuous sensor records. streamMetabolizer package fits models, input data usually need fair amount preparation first: timestamps must regular, times must converted mean solar time, site pressure must realistic, dissolved oxygen saturation must calculated actual water temperature pressure. vignette shows preparation step step using built-french_creek dataset. data come field study French Creek near Laramie, Wyoming, USA (Hotchkiss Hall 2015). runnable workflow uses modeled PAR calc_light(). optional chunk also shows retrieve observed light surface pressure NASA POWER working interactively internet connection.
code: library(preMetabolizer) library(dplyr) #>  #> Attaching package: 'dplyr' #> The following objects are masked from 'package:stats': #>  #>     filter, lag #> The following objects are masked from 'package:base': #>  #>     intersect, setdiff, setequal, union library(ggplot2)

title: Preparing French Creek data for stream metabolism modeling
text: dataset covers 15-minute intervals August 23 September 30, 2012. Two sensors deployed site: "REZN" ran start study September 1 12:50 MDT, replaced "TOWN". plot shows full record sensors.  REZN sensor shows cluster anomalous negative temperature values near end deployment. rest vignette filter TOWN sensor, longer cleaner record.
code: data(french_creek) french_creek <- french_creek |>    filter(lubridate::minute(datetime) %in% c(0, 15, 30, 45))  glimpse(french_creek) #> Rows: 3,629 #> Columns: 4 #> $ datetime <dttm> 2012-08-23 23:15:00, 2012-08-23 23:30:00, 2012-08-23 23:45:0… #> $ sonde    <chr> "REZN", "REZN", "REZN", "REZN", "REZN", "REZN", "REZN", "REZN… #> $ temp_C   <dbl> 14.13, 13.86, 13.60, 13.36, 13.19, 13.02, 12.88, 12.76, 12.61… #> $ DO_mgL   <dbl> 7.40, 7.30, 7.27, 7.25, 7.26, 7.28, 7.25, 7.23, 7.12, 7.03, 6… french_creek |>   tidyr::pivot_longer(     c(temp_C, DO_mgL),     names_to = "variable",     values_to = "value"   ) |>   mutate(variable = recode(     variable,     temp_C = "Temperature (°C)",     DO_mgL = "DO (mg/L)"   )) |>   ggplot(aes(datetime, value, color = sonde)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.3, alpha = 0.8) +   facet_wrap(~variable, ncol = 1, scales = "free_y") +   scale_color_manual(values = c(REZN = "#E69F00", TOWN = "#0072B2")) +   labs(x = NULL, y = NULL, color = "Sensor") +   theme_bw() #> Warning: Removed 272 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_line()`). french_creek <- french_creek |>   filter(sonde == "TOWN") |>   select(-sonde)

title: Preparing French Creek data for stream metabolism modeling
text: Negative water temperatures physically impossible liquid water indicate sensor malfunction. values <= 5 mg/L improbable may indicate sensor malfunction. ’ll remove rows.
code: french_creek <- french_creek |>   filter(is.na(temp_C) | temp_C >= 0) |>    filter(is.na(DO_mgL) | DO_mgL > 5)

title: Preparing French Creek data for stream metabolism modeling
text: streamMetabolizer requires evenly spaced data. even_timesteps() fills gaps NA rows interval consistent.
code: french_creek <- even_timesteps(french_creek, datetime_col = "datetime")

title: Preparing French Creek data for stream metabolism modeling
text: streamMetabolizer works mean solar time, shifts UTC longitude-based offset noon falls roughly sun highest. define site constants reuse throughout.
code: site_latitude <- 41.329678 site_longitude <- -106.359058  french_creek <- french_creek |>   mutate(     solar.time = convert_UTC_to_solartime(       datetime,       longitude = site_longitude,       time.type = "mean solar"     )   ) |>    filter(solar.time >= as.POSIXct("2012-09-18 04:05:58") &            solar.time <= as.POSIXct("2012-09-21 03:50:58"))

title: Preparing French Creek data for stream metabolism modeling
text: calc_light() returns photosynthetically active radiation µmol/m²/s based solar geometry site. takes mean solar time input.
code: french_creek <- french_creek |>   mutate(     light.calc = calc_light(       solar.time,       latitude = site_latitude,       longitude = site_longitude     )   )

title: Preparing French Creek data for stream metabolism modeling
text: French Creek sits roughly 3,205 m sea level. Using standard sea-level pressure (101.325 kPa) overpredict O2 saturation, adjust sea-level pressure site elevation correct_bp(). get_nasa_data() can download NASA POWER surface pressure (PSC) shortwave irradiance (ALLSKY_SFC_SW_DWN) site. converts shortwave irradiance observed PAR light.obs interpolates NASA fields timestamps logger data. can retrieve elevation directly USGS Elevation Point Query Service already know :
code: french_elev_m <- 3205  french_data <- french_creek |>   mutate(     bp_kPa = correct_bp(       station_bp = 101.325,       air_temp = temp_C,       station_elev = 0,       site_elev = french_elev_m     ),     light.obs = light.calc   ) nasa_dat <- get_nasa_data(   data = french_creek,   datetime_col = "datetime",   latitude = site_latitude,   longitude = site_longitude,   elev_m = french_elev_m,   params = c("PSC", "ALLSKY_SFC_SW_DWN") ) |>   select(datetime = dateTime, bp_kPa = PSC, light.obs)  french_data <- french_data |>   select(-bp_kPa, -light.obs) |>   left_join(nasa_dat, by = "datetime") french_elev_m <- get_usgs_elev(   latitude = site_latitude,   longitude = site_longitude,   units = "Meters" )

what: Optional: get pressure and observed light from NASA POWER
title: Preparing French Creek data for stream metabolism modeling
text: get_nasa_data() can download NASA POWER surface pressure (PSC) shortwave irradiance (ALLSKY_SFC_SW_DWN) site. converts shortwave irradiance observed PAR light.obs interpolates NASA fields timestamps logger data. can retrieve elevation directly USGS Elevation Point Query Service already know :
code: nasa_dat <- get_nasa_data(   data = french_creek,   datetime_col = "datetime",   latitude = site_latitude,   longitude = site_longitude,   elev_m = french_elev_m,   params = c("PSC", "ALLSKY_SFC_SW_DWN") ) |>   select(datetime = dateTime, bp_kPa = PSC, light.obs)  french_data <- french_data |>   select(-bp_kPa, -light.obs) |>   left_join(nasa_dat, by = "datetime") french_elev_m <- get_usgs_elev(   latitude = site_latitude,   longitude = site_longitude,   units = "Meters" )

title: Preparing French Creek data for stream metabolism modeling
text: site-adjusted pressure water temperature, calc_O2sat() returns saturation concentration mg/L.
code: french_data <- french_data |>   mutate(     DO.sat = calc_O2sat(       temp_water = temp_C,       atmo_press = bp_kPa,       units      = "kPa"     )   )  summary(french_data$DO.sat) #>    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max.  #>   7.134   7.626   8.239   8.129   8.624   8.970

title: Preparing French Creek data for stream metabolism modeling
text: final step restructures data retains observed calculated light.   fit model streamMetabolizer::metab(), choose either light.obs light.calc rename column light. See streamMetabolizer documentation details model fitting interpreting metabolism estimates.
code: sm_input <- french_data |>   transmute(     solar.time = solar.time,     DO.obs     = DO_mgL,     DO.sat     = DO.sat,     depth      = 0.16,     temp.water = temp_C,     light.obs  = light.obs,     light.calc = light.calc   )  head(sm_input) #> # A tibble: 6 × 7 #>   solar.time          DO.obs DO.sat depth temp.water light.obs light.calc #>   <dttm>               <dbl>  <dbl> <dbl>      <dbl>     <dbl>      <dbl> #> 1 2012-09-18 04:10:44   8.4    8.89  0.16       3.58         0          0 #> 2 2012-09-18 04:25:44   8.42   8.90  0.16       3.54         0          0 #> 3 2012-09-18 04:40:44   8.42   8.91  0.16       3.5          0          0 #> 4 2012-09-18 04:55:44   8.42   8.92  0.16       3.46         0          0 #> 5 2012-09-18 05:10:44   8.44   8.93  0.16       3.42         0          0 #> 6 2012-09-18 05:25:44   8.47   8.94  0.16       3.37         0          0 sm_input |>   mutate(DO.pctsat = 100 * DO.obs / DO.sat) |>   tidyr::pivot_longer(     c(DO.obs, DO.sat, DO.pctsat),     names_to = "variable",     values_to = "value"   ) |>   mutate(units = ifelse(variable == "DO.pctsat", "DO\n(% sat)", "DO\n(mg/L)")) |>   ggplot(aes(solar.time, value, color = variable)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.3) +   facet_grid(units ~ ., scales = "free_y") +   labs(x = "Solar time", y = NULL, color = "Variable") +   theme_bw() labels <- c(   depth      = "depth~(m)",   temp.water = "water~temp~(degree*C)",   light.obs  = "atop(observed~PAR, (mu*mol~m^{-2}~s^{-1}))",   light.calc = "atop(calculated~PAR, (mu*mol~m^{-2}~s^{-1}))" )  sm_input |>   tidyr::pivot_longer(     c(depth, temp.water, light.obs, light.calc),     names_to = "variable",     values_to = "value"   ) |>   mutate(     variable = ordered(       variable,       levels = c("depth", "temp.water", "light.obs", "light.calc")     )   ) |>   ggplot(aes(solar.time, value, color = variable)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.3) +   facet_grid(     variable ~ .,     scales = "free_y",     labeller = ggplot2::as_labeller(labels, label_parsed)   ) +   scale_color_manual(     values = c(       depth      = "#333333",       temp.water = "#e8000d",       light.obs  = "#f2a900",       light.calc = "#f5c542"     )   ) +   labs(x = "Solar time", y = NULL, color = "Variable") +   theme_bw()

title: Downloading GHCNh data
text: Global Historical Climatology Network hourly dataset, usually abbreviated GHCNh, provides station-based hourly meteorological observations NOAA. preMetabolizer separates workflow two steps: download_ghcnh() downloads compressed CSV files one station one years. read_ghcnh() reads downloaded files, standardizes timestamps common variable types, combines files, removes columns entirely missing. Note: Downloading data requires internet connection. download chunks vignette use #| eval: false run package installation.
code: library(preMetabolizer) library(dplyr) library(ggplot2)

title: Downloading GHCNh data
text: GHCNh downloads require station ID. already one, use NOAA station helpers find candidate stations near study site. See vignette("noaa", package = "preMetabolizer") detail station metadata distance searches.
code: konza_noaa <- closest_noaa_stations(   latitude = 39.1068806,   longitude = -96.6117151,   dist_km = 50,   state = "KS" )  station_id <- konza_noaa |>   arrange(distance_km) |>   pull(GHCND_ID) |>   first()  station_id

title: Downloading GHCNh data
text: download_ghcnh() downloads one compressed CSV file requested year. default, files saved preMetabolizer’s NOAA cache directory. can also provide output_dir want files live project. reproducible projects, often clearer use project directory. requested file already exists locally, download_ghcnh() skips reports skipped_downloads.
code: download_result <- download_ghcnh(   station_id = station_id,   years = 2022:2024 )  str(download_result) download_result <- download_ghcnh(   station_id = station_id,   years = 2022:2024,   output_dir = "data/ghcnh",   quiet = TRUE )  download_result

title: Downloading GHCNh data
text: Use read_ghcnh() either directory explicit vector files. combine = TRUE, default, successfully read files combined one data frame. returned object contains data small read summary. combined data hourly$data.
code: hourly <- read_ghcnh(directory = "data/ghcnh", quiet = TRUE)  hourly$files_read hourly$date_range hourly$stations  glimpse(hourly$data)

title: Downloading GHCNh data
text: example creates tiny CSV shape GHCNh file reads read_ghcnh(). illustrates standardization happens files read: DATE parsed UTC DateTime column. Temperature-like variables stored tenths degrees divided 10. -9999 sentinel values converted NA. Columns entirely missing removed combined result.
code: example_dir <- file.path(tempdir(), "ghcnh-example") dir.create(example_dir, showWarnings = FALSE)  example_file <- file.path(example_dir, "GHCNh_USW00000001_2024.csv")  readr::write_csv(   tibble::tibble(     Station_name = c("Example station", "Example station"),     Station_ID = c("USW00000001", "USW00000001"),     DATE = c("2024-06-01T00:00:00", "2024-06-01T01:00:00"),     temperature = c(215, 209),     dew_point_temperature = c(178, -9999),     relative_humidity = c(78, 81),     empty_column = c(NA, NA)   ),   example_file )  example_hourly <- read_ghcnh(directory = example_dir, quiet = TRUE)  example_hourly$data #> # A tibble: 2 × 6 #>   Station_name  DateTime            Station_ID temperature dew_point_temperature #>   <chr>         <dttm>              <chr>            <dbl>                 <dbl> #> 1 Example stat… 2024-06-01 00:00:00 USW000000…        21.5                  17.8 #> 2 Example stat… 2024-06-01 01:00:00 USW000000…        20.9                  NA   #> # ℹ 1 more variable: relative_humidity <dbl>

title: Downloading GHCNh data
text: files read, result regular data frame. example summarizes daily mean air temperature relative humidity local example data. real downloaded data, pattern can used prepare meteorology inputs, check station coverage, compare candidate stations fitting stream metabolism models.
code: daily <- example_hourly$data |>   mutate(date = as.Date(DateTime)) |>   group_by(date) |>   summarise(     temperature_C = mean(temperature, na.rm = TRUE),     relative_humidity = mean(relative_humidity, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  daily #> # A tibble: 1 × 3 #>   date       temperature_C relative_humidity #>   <date>             <dbl>             <dbl> #> 1 2024-06-01          21.2              79.5 daily_temperature <- hourly$data |>   mutate(date = as.Date(DateTime)) |>   group_by(date) |>   summarise(     temperature_C = mean(temperature, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups = "drop"   )  ggplot(daily_temperature, aes(date, temperature_C)) +   geom_line(color = "#2c7fb8") +   labs(     x = NULL,     y = "Mean temperature (degrees C)",     title = "Daily mean air temperature"   ) +   theme_bw()

title: Analyzing discharge at Kings Creek
text: vignette demonstrates several preMetabolizer utilities working daily streamflow records. use kings_discharge, built-dataset contains daily USGS discharge water temperature Kings Creek (monitoring location USGS-06879650) Konza Prairie Biological Station northeastern Kansas water year 2025 (2024-10-01 2025-09-30). Kings Creek well-studied tallgrass prairie stream highly episodic flow regime: many days record zero discharge, brief intense pulses following rainfall events.
code: library(preMetabolizer) library(dplyr) #>  #> Attaching package: 'dplyr' #> The following objects are masked from 'package:stats': #>  #>     filter, lag #> The following objects are masked from 'package:base': #>  #>     intersect, setdiff, setequal, union library(ggplot2)

title: Analyzing discharge at Kings Creek
text: data long format one row per date × parameter combination. pivot wide format row represents single day discharge (00060, cfs) water temperature (00010, °C) separate columns.

title: Analyzing discharge at Kings Creek
text: USGS reports discharge cubic feet per second (cfs). convert_flow() converts cfs, cubic meters per second (cms), liters per second (lps). comparison international literature add cms column.
code: kings <- kings |>   mutate(discharge_cms = convert_flow(discharge_cfs, from = "cfs", to = "cms"))  summary(kings[, c("discharge_cfs", "discharge_cms")]) #>  discharge_cfs      discharge_cms     #>  Min.   : 0.00000   Min.   :0.00000   #>  1st Qu.: 0.00000   1st Qu.:0.00000   #>  Median : 0.00000   Median :0.00000   #>  Mean   : 0.08759   Mean   :0.00248   #>  3rd Qu.: 0.00000   3rd Qu.:0.00000   #>  Max.   :25.10000   Max.   :0.71075

title: Analyzing discharge at Kings Creek
text: get_season() assigns meteorological season (Winter, Spring, Summer, Fall) date, useful stratified summaries plots.
code: kings <- kings |>   mutate(season = factor(     get_season(time),     levels = c("Winter", "Spring", "Summer", "Fall")   ))  count(kings, season) #> # A tibble: 4 × 2 #>   season     n #>   <fct>  <int> #> 1 Winter    89 #> 2 Spring    93 #> 3 Summer    94 #> 4 Fall      89

title: Analyzing discharge at Kings Creek
code: kings |>   tidyr::pivot_longer(     c(discharge_cms, temp_C),     names_to = "variable",     values_to = "value"   ) |>   mutate(variable = recode(     variable,     discharge_cms = "Discharge (m³/s)",     temp_C = "Temperature (°C)"   )) |>   ggplot(aes(time, value)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 0.4) +   facet_wrap(~variable, ncol = 1, scales = "free_y") +   labs(x = NULL, y = NULL) +   theme_bw()

title: Analyzing discharge at Kings Creek
text: flag_z() uses moving-window robust Z-score identify values deviate unusually local neighborhood. apply water temperature check sensor spikes data-entry errors.
code: z_result <- flag_z(kings$temp_C, width = 7, threshold = 3, return_z = TRUE)  kings <- kings |>   mutate(     temp_z = z_result$z,     temp_flag = z_result$flag   )  cat(   "Flagged temperature values:",   sum(!is.na(kings$temp_flag)),   "\n" ) #> Flagged temperature values: 9

title: Analyzing discharge at Kings Creek
text: flow duration curve (FDC) shows fraction time discharge equals exceeds given value. calc_exceedance_prob() implements Weibull plotting-position formula. Setting rm.zero = TRUE excludes days zero discharge ranking, curve reflects non-zero portion flow regime.
code: kings <- kings |>   mutate(exceed_prob = calc_exceedance_prob(discharge_cms, rm.zero = TRUE))  zero_pct <- mean(kings$discharge_cms == 0, na.rm = TRUE) * 100 cat(sprintf("Days with zero discharge: %.1f%%\n", zero_pct)) #> Days with zero discharge: 95.1% kings |>   filter(!is.na(exceed_prob)) |>   ggplot(aes(exceed_prob, discharge_cms, color = season)) +   geom_point(size = 1.5, alpha = 0.8) +   scale_x_continuous(     labels = \(x) paste0(round(x * 100), "%"),     limits = c(0, 1)   ) +   scale_y_log10() +   scale_color_manual(values = c(     Winter = "#4575b4",     Spring = "#74add1",     Summer = "#fdae61",     Fall   = "#d73027"   )) +   labs(     x     = "Exceedance probability",     y     = "Discharge (m³/s)",     color = "Season"   ) +   theme_bw()

title: Analyzing discharge at Kings Creek
text: calc_cv() computes coefficient variation (CV), normalized measure variability. high CV indicates flashy, episodic regime; low CV indicates stable baseflow conditions.
code: kings |>   filter(discharge_cms > 0) |>   group_by(season) |>   summarise(     n_days   = n(),     mean_cms = mean(discharge_cms, na.rm = TRUE),     cv_pct   = calc_cv(discharge_cms, na.rm = TRUE, as_percent = TRUE),     .groups  = "drop"   ) #> # A tibble: 1 × 4 #>   season n_days mean_cms cv_pct #>   <fct>   <int>    <dbl>  <dbl> #> 1 Summer     18   0.0503   328.

title: Analyzing discharge at Kings Creek
text: calc_bin_width() suggests histogram bin width using classical rules. "fd" (Freedman-Diaconis) method robust outliers works well right-skewed flow data.
code: non_zero_q <- kings |> filter(discharge_cms > 0)  bw <- calc_bin_width(non_zero_q$discharge_cms, method = "fd")  ggplot(non_zero_q, aes(discharge_cms, fill = season)) +   geom_histogram(binwidth = bw, color = "white", linewidth = 0.2) +   scale_fill_manual(values = c(     Winter = "#4575b4",     Spring = "#74add1",     Summer = "#fdae61",     Fall   = "#d73027"   )) +   labs(     x    = "Discharge (m³/s)",     y    = "Days",     fill = "Season"   ) +   theme_bw()

title: Downloading Kansas Mesonet data
text: Kansas Mesonet statewide network automated weather stations operated Kansas State University. provides sub-hourly meteorological data — including air temperature, relative humidity, precipitation, solar radiation, wind, soil measurements — 60 stations across Kansas. vignette walks workflow discovering stations, browsing variable catalog, downloading data, reading cached files using preMetabolizer Mesonet functions. use Konza Prairie, long-term ecological research (LTER) site northeastern Kansas, example station. Note: Functions contact Mesonet API require internet connection. Code chunks marked #| eval: false run package installation. Run interactively session.
code: library(preMetabolizer) library(dplyr) library(ggplot2)

title: Downloading Kansas Mesonet data
text: ks_meso_vars() retrieves parses current Kansas Mesonet variable metadata, including CSV headings, variable names, units, descriptions. Search catalog find variables interest:
code: mesonet_vars <- ks_meso_vars() mesonet_vars |>   filter(grepl("temp|precip|solar|humidity", desc, ignore.case = TRUE)) |>   select(var, tidy_name, units, desc)

title: Downloading Kansas Mesonet data
text: ks_meso_stations() retrieves metadata Mesonet stations, including location, network affiliation, whether station supports FW13 fire weather reports. Filter find Konza Prairie confirm exact station name used API: station name recognized API "Konza Prairie".
code: stations <- ks_meso_stations() glimpse(stations) konza <- stations |>   filter(grepl("Konza", StationName, ignore.case = TRUE))  konza

title: Downloading Kansas Mesonet data
text: ks_meso_station_activity() shows observation intervals available station date range archived data. useful verifying station data study period identifying finest available temporal resolution.
code: activity <- ks_meso_station_activity()  activity |>   filter(station == "Konza Prairie")

title: Downloading Kansas Mesonet data
text: ks_meso_most_recent() returns timestamp latest ingested observation every station given interval, helpful monitoring live data pipelines.
code: recent <- ks_meso_most_recent(interval = "hour")  recent |>   filter(station == "Konza Prairie")

title: Downloading Kansas Mesonet data
text: get_ks_meso() downloads Mesonet data one stations saves result local cache directory. Large date ranges split chunks automatically stay within API record limit. download hourly data Konza Prairie 2024 calendar year, requesting air temperature, relative humidity, precipitation, surface pressure.
code: download_result <- get_ks_meso(   stations   = "Konza Prairie",   start_date = "2024-01-01",   end_date   = "2024-12-31",   interval   = "hour",   vars       = c("TEMP2MAVG", "RELHUM2MAVG", "PRECIP", "PRESSUREAVG") )  str(download_result)

title: Downloading Kansas Mesonet data
text: downloaded, read_ks_meso() reads cached file back R. TIMESTAMP column automatically parsed POSIXct Mesonet timezone (Etc/GMT+6, .e., CST without daylight saving).
code: konza_hourly <- read_ks_meso(   station    = "Konza Prairie",   start_date = "2024-01-01",   end_date   = "2024-12-31",   interval   = "hour" )  glimpse(konza_hourly)

title: Downloading Kansas Mesonet data
text: data hand, standard dplyr ggplot2 workflows apply. example summarizes monthly precipitation totals daily temperature ranges.
code: monthly_precip <- konza_hourly |>   mutate(month = lubridate::floor_date(TIMESTAMP, "month")) |>   group_by(month) |>   summarise(     precip_mm    = sum(PRECIP, na.rm = TRUE),     temp_mean_C  = mean(TEMP2MAVG, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups      = "drop"   )  ggplot(monthly_precip, aes(month, precip_mm)) +   geom_col(fill = "#4575b4") +   labs(     x = NULL,     y = "Precipitation (mm)",     title = "Monthly precipitation — Konza Prairie 2024"   ) +   theme_bw()

title: Downloading Kansas Mesonet data
text: ks_meso_fw13() retrieves fire weather records standard FW13 format used USDA Forest Service fire behavior modeling tools.
code: fw13_records <- ks_meso_fw13(   station    = "Konza Prairie",   start_date = "2024-04-01",   end_date   = "2024-04-30" )  # Each element is one fixed-width FW13 record head(fw13_records, 3)

title: Finding NOAA stations
text: NOAA station metadata often first thing need preparing meteorological inputs stream metabolism models. station table tells stations exist near study site, station identifiers available, whether station coordinates can used distance searches. preMetabolizer provides two helpers workflow: get_noaa_stations() downloads cleans station metadata NOAA’s Meteorological Station Historical Repository. closest_noaa_stations() finds stations within distance target latitude longitude. Note: functions contact NOAA first time need station metadata. downloaded file cached locally reused later calls. Code chunks marked #| eval: false intended run interactively.
code: library(preMetabolizer) library(dplyr) library(ggplot2)

title: Finding NOAA stations
text: Use get_noaa_stations() retrieve cleaned NOAA station table. cleaned table removes records without usable coordinates, filters balloon platforms, groups records GHCND_ID. need stations one U.S. state, use state argument. State codes case-insensitive.
code: stations <- get_noaa_stations(debug = FALSE)  glimpse(stations) ks_stations <- get_noaa_stations(state = "KS", debug = FALSE)  ks_stations |>   select(GHCND_ID, NAME_PRINCIPAL, LAT_DEC, LON_DEC, ELEV_GROUND) |>   arrange(NAME_PRINCIPAL)

title: Finding NOAA stations
text: site-level work, usually easier start latitude longitude. closest_noaa_stations() calculates geodesic distance target coordinate station returns stations within search radius. example searches within 50 km Konza Prairie Biological Station. state argument optional, can make searches faster easier inspect know site far state border.
code: konza_noaa <- closest_noaa_stations(   latitude = 39.1068806,   longitude = -96.6117151,   dist_km = 50,   state = "KS" )  konza_noaa |>   select(distance_km, GHCND_ID, NAME_PRINCIPAL, LAT_DEC, LON_DEC) |>   arrange(distance_km)

title: Finding NOAA stations
text: useful pattern try narrow search radius first, widen stations variables time span need. can also map candidates choosing station download.
code: near_25 <- closest_noaa_stations(   latitude = 39.1068806,   longitude = -96.6117151,   dist_km = 25,   state = "KS" )  near_100 <- closest_noaa_stations(   latitude = 39.1068806,   longitude = -96.6117151,   dist_km = 100,   state = "KS" )  list(   within_25_km = nrow(near_25),   within_100_km = nrow(near_100) ) ggplot(konza_noaa, aes(LON_DEC, LAT_DEC)) +   geom_point(aes(size = distance_km), color = "#2c7fb8") +   geom_point(     aes(x = -96.6117151, y = 39.1068806),     inherit.aes = FALSE,     color = "#d95f0e",     size = 3   ) +   coord_quickmap() +   labs(     x = "Longitude",     y = "Latitude",     size = "Distance (km)",     title = "NOAA stations near Konza Prairie"   ) +   theme_bw()

what: Use station IDs with GHCNh data
title: Finding NOAA stations
text: GHCND_ID column identifies stations NOAA’s Global Historical Climatology Network. hourly GHCNh downloads, pass one station ID download_ghcnh(). station ID, see vignette("ghcnh", package = "preMetabolizer") downloading reading hourly observations.
code: station_id <- konza_noaa |>   arrange(distance_km) |>   pull(GHCND_ID) |>   first()  station_id

what: A Utility Package for the streamMetabolizer Package
title: A Utility Package for the streamMetabolizer Package
text: preMetabolizer helps turn field public environmental data regular, physically consistent inputs needed stream metabolism models. designed work usually happens streamMetabolizer: finding nearby weather stations, downloading meteorological elevation data, standardizing timestamps, converting units, calculating light, pressure, dissolved gas, water property variables. package useful need : prepare dissolved oxygen, water temperature, depth, light, pressure inputs metabolism models; fill timestamp gaps logger data even time steps; convert common hydrology atmospheric units without adding units dependency workflow; retrieve NOAA, NASA POWER, USGS elevation, Kansas Mesonet data study site; make quick quality-control checks moving-window outlier flags, flow-duration curves, seasonal summaries.

title: A Utility Package for the streamMetabolizer Package
text: preMetabolizer CRAN. Install development version GitHub: remotes: package requires R (>= 4.1.0). companion fork streamMetabolizer listed Remotes: installed automatically.
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("ConnorB/preMetabolizer") # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("ConnorB/preMetabolizer")

title: A Utility Package for the streamMetabolizer Package
text: workflows begin logger data contain timestamp, observed dissolved oxygen, water temperature. example uses built-French Creek dataset builds core columns expected streamMetabolizer::metab(). complete version workflow, including observed light NASA POWER, see vignette("french-creek", package = "preMetabolizer").
code: library(preMetabolizer) library(dplyr)  data(french_creek)  site_latitude <- 41.329678 site_longitude <- -106.359058 site_elev_m <- 3205  metab_input <- french_creek |>   filter(sonde == "TOWN") |>   select(datetime, DO_mgL, temp_C) |>   even_timesteps(datetime_col = "datetime") |>   mutate(     solar.time = convert_UTC_to_solartime(       datetime,       longitude = site_longitude     ),     light = calc_light(       solar.time,       latitude = site_latitude,       longitude = site_longitude     ),     bp_kPa = correct_bp(       station_bp = 101.325,       air_temp = temp_C,       station_elev = 0,       site_elev = site_elev_m     ),     DO.sat = calc_O2sat(temp_C, bp_kPa, units = "kPa")   ) |>   transmute(     solar.time,     DO.obs = DO_mgL,     DO.sat,     depth = 0.16,     temp.water = temp_C,     light   )  head(metab_input)

title: A Utility Package for the streamMetabolizer Package
code: library(preMetabolizer)  # Dissolved oxygen saturation at 15°C, standard atmosphere, freshwater calc_O2sat(temp_water = 15, atmo_press = 1, units = "atm") #> [1] 10.07807  # Water density (kg/m³) at several temperatures calc_water_density(water_temp = c(0, 10, 20, 30)) #> [1] 999.8395 999.6996 998.2041 995.6473  # Barometric pressure correction from a station at 300 m to a site at 500 m correct_bp(station_bp = 101.3, air_temp = 15, station_elev = 300, site_elev = 500) #> [1] 98.92626  # Dissolved CO2 concentration from xCO2, pressure, and water depth calc_CO2_mgL(   CO2_ppm = 420,   temp_water = 20,   waterDepth_m = 0.5,   atmo_press = 101.325,   press_units = "kPa" )

title: A Utility Package for the streamMetabolizer Package
code: # Stream discharge: cfs → cms convert_flow(14.32, from = "cfs", to = "cms") #> [1] 0.4054972  # Barometric pressure: kPa → atm convert_pressure(101.325, from = "kPa", to = "atm") #> [1] 1

code: # Convert a UTC timestamp to mean solar time at a site in Kansas utc <- as.POSIXct("2024-06-21 18:00:00", tz = "UTC") solar <- convert_UTC_to_solartime(utc, longitude = -96.6, time.type = "mean solar")  # Model PAR near local solar noon on the summer solstice noon_solar <- convert_UTC_to_solartime(   as.POSIXct("2024-06-21 18:00:00", tz = "UTC"),   longitude = -96.6 ) calc_light(noon_solar, latitude = 39.1, longitude = -96.6) #> [1] 2228.291

title: Calculate histogram bin width — calc_bin_width
text: Computes suggested bin width histogram using one several classical rules. NA values silently removed computation.

title: Calculate histogram bin width — calc_bin_width
code: calc_bin_width(x, method = "auto")

title: Calculate histogram bin width — calc_bin_width
text: x Numeric vector observations. Must contain least two distinct, finite, non-NA values. method Single character string specifying binning rule. One : "auto" Minimum Freedman-Diaconis Sturges (default). Tends work well across wide range distributions. "sturges" Sturges' rule: \(h = R / (\log_2 n + 1)\). Assumes approximate normality; tends undersmooth large \(n\). "fd" Freedman-Diaconis: \(h = 2 \cdot \mathrm{IQR}(x)     \cdot n^{-1/3}\). Robust outliers. Falls back Sturges \(\mathrm{IQR}(x) = 0\). "sqrt" Square-root rule: \(h = R / \sqrt{n}\). Simple heuristic used spreadsheet applications. "rice" Rice rule: \(h = R / (2 n^{1/3})\). Similar Sturges grows slowly. "scott" Scott's rule: \(h = 3.49 \hat\sigma n^{-1/3}\). Optimal normal data sense minimising mean integrated squared error. "doane" Doane's rule. Extends Sturges account skewness; better suited non-normal distributions. Requires \(n \ge 3\).

title: Calculate histogram bin width — calc_bin_width
text: single positive numeric value giving bin width.

title: Calculate histogram bin width — calc_bin_width
text: formulae, \(R\) denotes range (diff(range(x))) \(n\) number non-NA observations. "fd" fallback Sturges IQR(x) == 0 avoids zero-width bin, can occur heavily discrete zero-inflated data.

title: Calculate histogram bin width — calc_bin_width
text: function aborts informative message : x numeric vector. method single character string. x contains non-NA values, one. non-NA values x identical (zero range). method = "doane" requested fewer 3 observations (skewness standard error undefined \(n < 3\)). unrecognised method string supplied.

title: Calculate histogram bin width — calc_bin_width
code: # Continuous data — Freedman-Diaconis calc_bin_width(rnorm(200), "fd") #> [1] 0.448634  # Skewed data — Doane corrects for skewness calc_bin_width(rexp(200), "doane") #> [1] 0.3778829  # Discrete / zero-inflated data — FD falls back to Sturges calc_bin_width(c(rep(0, 50), rep(1, 50)), "fd") #> [1] 0.4308869  # NA values are silently dropped calc_bin_width(c(NA, rnorm(100), NA), "scott") #> [1] 0.7285563  # Compare all methods on the same data x <- rnorm(500) methods <- c("auto", "sturges", "fd", "sqrt", "rice", "scott", "doane") vapply(methods, \(m) calc_bin_width(x, m), numeric(1)) #>      auto   sturges        fd      sqrt      rice     scott     doane  #> 0.3280859 0.5811504 0.3280859 0.2590091 0.3648492 0.4261072 0.5560889   # Error conditions try(calc_bin_width(letters))               # non-numeric x #> Error in calc_bin_width(letters) : `x` must be a numeric vector. #> ✖ Got an object of class <character>. try(calc_bin_width(c(1, NA, NA)))          # only one non-NA value #> Error in calc_bin_width(c(1, NA, NA)) :  #>   `x` contains only one non-"NA" value. #> ℹ At least two observations are required to compute a bin width. try(calc_bin_width(rep(5, 50)))            # zero range #> Error in calc_bin_width(rep(5, 50)) :  #>   `x` has zero range: all non-"NA" values are identical. #> ℹ Bin width is undefined when all observations are the same. try(calc_bin_width(rnorm(200), "badrule")) # unknown method #> Error in calc_bin_width(rnorm(200), "badrule") :  #>   Invalid `method` argument. #> ✖ Method "badrule" is not supported. #> ℹ Allowed methods: "auto", "sturges", "fd", "sqrt", "rice", "scott", and #>   "doane" try(calc_bin_width(c(1, 2), "doane"))      # doane needs n >= 3 #> Error in calc_bin_width(c(1, 2), "doane") :  #>   Doane's rule requires at least 3 observations. #> ✖ `x` has only 2 non-"NA" values after removing "NA"s. #> ℹ Use a different `method` or supply more data.

title: preMetabolizer 0.0.0.9000
text: Added french_creek dataset: 5-minute dissolved oxygen water temperature records French Creek, Laramie, WY (Aug–Sep 2012), courtesy Bob Hall. Source: Hall et al. (2016), . Added “Preparing French Creek Data Stream Metabolism Modeling” vignette demonstrating complete preMetabolizer workflow: solar time conversion, modeled PAR, elevation-corrected barometric pressure, O2 saturation, streamMetabolizer input assembly. calc_O2sat() now uses Benson Krause umol/kg coefficients Garcia Gordon (1992), converts mg/L salinity-aware density, applies corrected vapor-pressure term non-standard pressure (#TBD). calc_vapor_press() method = "MIMSY" now returns physically correct values. Antoine equation constant B incorrectly set 140.264 (1435.264), causing vapor pressures orders magnitude large. calc_water_density() longer accepts .drop_units argument; always returns plain numeric vector (#TBD). calc_water_height() now gives clear error type = "unvented" used without providing atmo_kPa, rather silently returning NA. closest_noaa_stations() now uses latitude longitude arguments. old lat, long, lon aliases deprecated (#TBD). convert_flow() now requires explicit argument specifying input unit; always returns plain numeric vector instead units object (#TBD). convert_PAR_to_SW() convert_SW_to_PAR() longer re-exported preMetabolizer.
